steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (textual) question asking what claim the passage makes about petroleum demand. The decisive evidence is the clause: “as social attitudes increasingly favor using less carbon-intensive sources of energy, demand for petroleum falls.” The response must reflect this stated relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Companies involved in petroleum extraction are not affected by the demand for petroleum.: Contradicts the passage, which says falling petroleum prices make it harder to recover equipment costs — companies are directly affected. ✗
  • Choice B — The societal preference for low-carbon energy sources is a factor in the demand for petroleum.: Matches the passage exactly: as social attitudes favor low-carbon energy, demand for petroleum falls. ✓
  • Choice C — The demand for petroleum follows a predictable annual cycle.: The passage never mentions a cycle. Demand falls as social attitudes shift — no cyclical pattern is described. ✗
  • Choice D — Companies involved in petroleum extraction can benefit from a decline in the demand for petroleum.: Contradicts the passage, which states that falling demand causes drilling equipment to “become a liability” — a harm, not a benefit. ✗
B is correct.
